title: Chapter Eighty-Nine: What Determines Worth
content:
## JEONG YEON-SHIN

The strange company formed like a blade finding its sheath -- reluctantly, but with a certain grim precision.

Namgung Mi walked between them through Wuchang's streets, wearing dark blue light armour that made her look like a palace maiden descended to the mortal world. The crowds fell silent as they passed. Word had spread. The touching story of the Flourishing Spirit Flower Master who lost her guard warriors to a Blood Flame Sect noble. The tale of White Qilin and Lightning Genius welcoming their life-and-death opponent as a companion to protect.

*Wuchang's people admired composure. What they really admired was theatre.*

"They say your title is Lightning Genius?" Namgung Mi said. Her tone was flat. Regal. The way one addresses a servant whose name one has condescended to learn.

Yeon-shin did not answer.

They had already exchanged harsh moves -- he had seized her by the throat and sealed her blood points while wearing a demon mask. It felt grotesque to make small talk with a woman who did not know she was speaking to the man who had humiliated her.

Besides, she would die soon. He did not particularly want to exchange words with the dead.

"Now even these bastards provoke my mood?" she said, lips crooked. She walked with mincing steps, the dark blue hem of her armour swaying. Even as a prisoner in all but name, she carried herself like royalty.

*Born that way. Raised like it. Built her own solid world through Namgung direct-line blood.*

Yeon-shin glanced at her once and looked away.

*Worthless.*

"Namgung Mi." Hwa-sin's voice was tight. "Keep courtesy in your speech. Young Hero Jeong is the Fortress Lord's direct disciple. His status is no less noble than yours."

"Our Hwa-sin." The corners of her mouth curled. "Your jokes have grown more than your martial arts." She turned that smile on Yeon-shin. "A disciple of a monstrous divine being barely clinging to life is *noble*? Unless the word precious has become laughable."

Hwa-sin closed his mouth. His expression was heavy. To Yeon-shin's eyes, he looked very soft -- a man who yielded even in small arguments while walking toward his mother's enemy. Perhaps the loyalty brainwashing from his childhood as Sword Night's young master had never truly left him.

Namgung Mi turned to Yeon-shin with a smirk. "You too, little one. You probably haven't received all the true divine martial arts? What's different from our Eight Families where blood kin is everything? You pitiful half-bastard."

"Eight Families sounds strange," Yeon-shin said.

"What?"

"Now it's Seven Families. No -- Six Families might sound better. What do you think? They're just things that easily decrease."

Her eyes widened. Her mouth opened for a dismissive retort, but he cut through it quietly, the way a blade parts silk.

"Maintain the meaning of the formal martial matches. Your Namgung surname is the only reason we show you courtesy. Our level for comparing martial arts depends on our opponent. No weight to one who will contest life and death with me? How empty that is."

Silence.

"If you don't understand, feel free to babble as you wish. It would just mean that's the extent of your capacity."

He turned his head away. He caught a glimpse of her fine features twisted with something he had not seen before -- not rage, not contempt, but the beginning of doubt. He thought the conversation had been excessively long.

*She wasn't worth ten breaths of my time.*

"A half-bastard speaks of capacity?" she sneered, and then her mouth fell quiet.

She was still a daughter of a renowned martial family. She had nothing left to say to a man who spoke of level.

Afterward, something shifted. The way she looked at him changed -- the same proud downward gaze, but threaded with something else. Examination. Appraisal. As if she were seeing him for the first time.

He thought of the demon mask and dismissed it. The Blood Flame Sect noble and Radiant Demon Wing's Lightning Genius were different people. Different tones. Different intent.

*Nothing to worry about.*

He apologized to Hwa-sin for the remark about the Namgung name.

"No, it's fine." The White Qilin waved his hand with a cheerful laugh. "Rather satisfying in its own way."

Both of them ignored Namgung Mi's snort.

---

The road to Huizhou in South Zhili ran through Huangzhou -- three main roads crossing at a city where merchant groups and martial artists flowed like rivers through a delta. The inn they chose was large and bustling, full of men who spoke too loudly about things they understood poorly.

"Desolate Sect versus Namgung martial matches! Where else could we see martial arts of such lofty masters?"

"Namgung should win, though. If Desolate Sect fights well, even Huangzhou's market prices would shake."

"The young master is the young master. How can we measure him with words like *rising talent*?"

Namgung Mi's lips curved as she listened. She leaned toward Yeon-shin with a faint smile.

"Their words aren't all wrong," she said. "To your eyes that only see what's near, our Namgung might not seem different from evil sects. But to commoners, we're truly an orthodox sect unmatched under heaven. When floods come, we release food. When evil sects cause trouble, Namgung warriors step forward."

Yeon-shin said nothing. He turned a page of the Diamond Sutra.

"Rising talent level can't receive the main family's Emperor Sword Form. My brother has trained it to a level hard to estimate. Isn't that enough reason for your defeat?" The hazy smile deepened. "I mean you can peacefully close your eyes under my brother's sword."

"Isn't your mouth tired?" he said. "Speaking meaningless words."

"Or." Her expression turned strange -- white face, dark eyes, the ghost of something sharp beneath the porcelain surface. "Surrender first and throw yourself to the main family. I'll let you just wash my feet. I mean it."

Washing feet.

The same words he had spoken wearing the demon mask.

*Is she testing me?*

Or was it just vicious behaviour with no particular meaning? He was still turning the question when the air tore apart.

WHOOSH!

Dozens of sharp wind streams blew in through the entrance. Black wind carrying a human figure. Energy waves that felt like they would rip through skin and bone.

Yeon-shin and Hwa-sin moved simultaneously -- not drawing swords, but raising true qi first, because the power rushing toward them demanded nothing less.

But the intruder was not aimed at them.

BOOM!

Hair black as ebony, distorted with transparent gloss. A hand extending like a swift sword seized both of Namgung Mi's cheeks -- fingers as white as hers -- and lifted her into the air.

"You," the Seventh Apostle said, red lips drawing a smooth arc. "What did you say?"

---
